_meta.generator: Wave 28 AI registry deeper attack (Smithery+Glama 24-72h elapsed verify + mcpservers.org mail SENT + mcphub.io/.dev probe + alternative platform discovery)

platforms.name: Official MCP Registry (Anthropic / modelcontextprotocol.io)
platforms.publish_method: mcp-publisher publish (github auth via OIDC, see .github/workflows/mcp-registry-publish.yml)
platforms.next_action: publish v0.3.5 once PyPI 0.3.5 artifact lands (registry validates PyPI 404 -> 400). PR #55 / release.yml in retry monitor.

platforms.name: Smithery (smithery.ai)
platforms.http_status_w28: 429 (rate-limited via WebFetch) / 308 redirect to /servers/@bookyou/jpcite via curl
platforms.next_action: wait until 72h elapsed (2026-05-14 ~16:00 UTC). If still 404, re-fire workflow + Discord escalate.

platforms.name: Glama (glama.ai)
platforms.api_probe_w28: https://glama.ai/api/mcp/v1/servers?q=jpcite|autonomath -> 0 matches (returns generic 10-server page, search param ignored)
platforms.next_action: Discord webhook URL not publicly exposed. Operator paste-verbatim via docs/_internal/mcp_registry_submissions/glama_discord_escalation.md. Alternative: comment update on awesome-mcp PR #6192 (Wave 28).
platforms.pr_6192_state: OPEN MERGEABLE (4 comments, last 2026-05-11 14:41 UTC); glama-bot blocking on Glama listing.

platforms.name: PulseMCP (pulsemcp.com)
platforms.submission_method: auto-ingest from Anthropic Official Registry (daily) + weekly process
platforms.next_action: wait until 2026-05-20 elapses. Mailer ready: tools/offline/submit_pulsemcp_mail.py.

platforms.name: mcphub.dev
platforms.submit_form_note: Form mentions 'Direct form submissions are coming soon' and points to GitHub repo aswbmw/mcp-hub for PR submission. BUT https://github.com/aswbmw/mcp-hub returns 404 (repo deleted/private).
platforms.submission_blocker: PR fallback path broken (target repo 404). No alternative submission channel visible.
platforms.next_action: drop from active tracking until aswbmw/mcp-hub re-emerges or alternative submission channel published.

platforms.name: mcphub.io
platforms.title: MCP Hub - MCP Server Search & Discover & Collection Website
platforms.probe_result: Pure client-side rendered SPA (BAILOUT_TO_CLIENT_SIDE_RENDERING). No server-side submit form or API endpoint visible. Browse + search only.
platforms.next_action: no submit channel available. Drop unless a future revision adds a submission API.

platforms.name: mcphub.dev vs mcphub.io brand separation
platforms.verdict: mcphub.io = MCP Hub (Vercel SPA, browse-only, no submit API). mcphub.dev = MCP Hub directory (CF-hosted Next.js, submit-form-only with broken GitHub PR fallback). Different operators, both unsubmittable as of W28.
platforms.next_action: track both as dead-end submission paths until either ships a working endpoint.

platforms.name: mcp.so
platforms.submission_method: https://mcp.so/submit (free form; paid $39 expedite — NOT used per zero-touch organic-only policy)
platforms.next_action: re-poll in 7d (2026-05-19). If still placeholder, submit via free form via operator-paste.

platforms.name: punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers PR #6192
platforms.reviewer_blocker: glama-bot requires Glama listing live before merge (badge URL must resolve)
platforms.next_action: Wave 28: gated on Glama listing. Plan Wave 29 follow-up comment if Glama still 404 by 2026-05-15.

platforms.name: mcpservers.org (community catalog)
platforms.submission_method: email contact@mcpservers.org via xrea SMTP (CF email-protection decoded Wave 23)
platforms.mail_subject: [mcp server submission] jpcite — Japanese public-program evidence
platforms.next_action: wait. If no listing by 2026-05-26, resend mail or escalate via GitHub issue on backing repo.

platforms.name: cursor.directory MCP store

platforms.name: Claude.ai external plugins (Anthropic)
platforms.note: Wave 23 confirmed LIVE via Wave 16 submission flow (submit_claude_project_mail.py + draft).

platforms.name: mcp.run -> turbomcp.ai
platforms.verdict: domain repurposed to enterprise self-hosted gateway. Sales-led. Not a public catalog.

platforms.name: modelcontextprotocol/servers README resources section
platforms.policy: README only links to community awesome-lists; direct PR to add server is rejected — register at registry.modelcontextprotocol.io instead
platforms.next_action: no action; covered by Anthropic Official Registry (LIVE).

platforms.name: mcpregistry.io
platforms.http_status: timeout (28s+ x 3 attempts, Wave 20 + Wave 22 + W23)
platforms.verdict: domain non-responsive; drop from active tracking.

platforms.name: mcpregistry.org (W28 alt-domain probe)
platforms.http_status_w28: 405 Method Not Allowed (no GET handler; possibly API-only with POST or webhook receiver)
platforms.verdict: no public catalog / no documented submission path.

platforms.name: mcpfinder.com (W28 new discovery)
platforms.title: Model Context Protocol Finder - Discover AI Development Tools
platforms.submit_path: /submit (Google Sign-in gated form; no public API endpoint visible)
platforms.directory_state: empty ('No Model Context Protocols found' visible W28)
platforms.next_action: low priority. Empty catalog + Google-auth-gated submit = high friction for low signal. Re-poll Q3 2026.

platforms.name: mcp-explorer.com (W28 new discovery)
platforms.title: MCP Explorer: The Developer Tool for MCP Servers
platforms.verdict: desktop application (macOS/Windows/Linux) for testing/debugging MCP servers, NOT a directory or catalog. Out of scope for registry submission.

platforms.name: smithery.dev (W28 alt-domain probe)
platforms.verdict: alive domain. Not the canonical Smithery (which is smithery.ai). Treat as parked/unrelated until evidence shows otherwise.
